notes: Opportunity openings:plaintiff. Objective: opening statement. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Only visible evidence is case-packet ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, 4971 bytes. It proves operative market rule, examples, source hierarchy, and metadata, but not the underlying airspace event. Rule elements: Iran initiated closure; non-weather; by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET; major means broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing Iranian airspace or major region, or complete non-weather suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports; limited exceptions do not disqualify, limited partial closures do not qualify; primary sources official Iranian aviation authorities and consensus credible reporting. Investigation/source map: searched broad and exact phrases for Iran May 24/May 22-25 2026 airspace closure, Reuters, NOTAM, CAA, Flightradar24. Leads found outside record: SafeAirspace/Opsgroup context re Iran partial reopening/western part closed after conflict; several secondary/social mirrors point to possible NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 effective May 22-May 25 closing western Tehran FIR/all but limited western airports/daylight/permission. These are not admitted and should be treated as later evidence targets only. Adverse issues: defense may argue partial/western restrictions are not 'major' under rule, or that non-Iran advisories/operator avoidance are insufficient, or that May 22 action did not initiate a new qualifying closure before deadline if a prior closure/reopening regime was already in effect. Need later: capture official NOTAM/CAA text if available; confirm UTC validity through May 25 03:59 UTC; map airports affected, including at least two listed airports or major-region coverage; preserve credible reporting/flight-tracking as evidence. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant opening work notes. Opportunity `openings:defendant`, text limit 5000, target <=3750. Inspected current record via get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Only visible evidence is case-packet `ev_e61df84283cc_market-page`, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, text/plain, 4971 bytes. It proves the market rule and source standard, not any underlying May 24 closure event. Rule elements: Iran initiated; non-weather; by May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET; major closure. Major closure requires broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region, or a complete non-weather suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Limited cancellations/delays/partial closures and foreign or airline restrictions do not qualify. Plaintiff opening concedes outside evidence not admitted yet and says the source chain should be official Iranian aviation material plus credible reporting.

Native off-record investigation before opening: searches run for `Iran airspace closure May 24 2026 NOTAM commercial flights`, `site:notaminfo.com OIIX May 24 2026 Iran airspace closed`, `Iran closes airspace May 24 2026 flights suspended Reuters`, and `"Iran closes its airspace" "May 24" "2026"`. Opened SafeAirspace Iran page and OpsGroup Middle East operational picture. Leads found: SafeAirspace describes June 2026 partial reopening, March 2026 OIIX/Tehran FIR closure after Feb 28 strikes, and foreign warnings; OpsGroup describes Iran in recovery period, some overflights possible, western Iran restricted, April 21 eastern reopening, and limited Tehran flight resumption April 24-26. Search/social snippets allege May 22-25 NOTAM/western FIR closure or western airports affected, but snippets/social posts are not reliable proof and are not admitted. Reuters/Facebook snippet suggests Jan 2026 broad closure with permission exceptions; packet already uses Jan as example, not May 24 event. No evidence admission allowed in openings, so opening will not cite these sources as proof.

Defense source map for later phases: primary targets are Iranian Civil Aviation Organization / Iran Airports and Air Navigation Company NOTAMs/orders around May 22-25, airport notices for IKA/THR/MHD/SYZ/IFN, NOTAM databases for OIIX/Tehran FIR, and official airport status records. Confirming sources: aviation risk databases, OpsGroup/SafeAirspace, FlightRadar24/ADS-B summaries, Reuters/credible reporting. Strong adverse source to test: any official Iranian NOTAM closing the entire OIIX/Tehran FIR or a major region to commercial flights around May 22-25, including prior-permission exceptions. Defense-favorable sources include foreign advisories, airline reroutes, partial reopening/partial restrictions, later June measures, or market-price commentary, none of which alone proves a qualifying Iran-initiated May 24 closure.

Opening theory: current record contains no event proof; plaintiff must connect an Iran-origin official action to the precise rule. Key distinctions are source (Iran vs foreign/airline), scope (major vs partial/local), covered operations (commercial transit/arrivals/departures or two listed airport suspensions), reason (non-weather), and deadline. Self-audit: need official NOTAM/order text or faithful capture before arguing specific May 24 facts; need adverse check against any CAA Iran notice closing OIIX or all western airports May 22-25; need distinguish ongoing March closure/recovery-period restrictions from a May 24 qualifying initiation.

Arguments phase notes. Record refreshed: only initial market-page case-packet before plaintiff submissions; defendant opening stresses source/scope/time and argues partial/foreign/advisory material insufficient. Evidence submitted/admitted: ev_a6870de8ffed_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-a6870de8ffed, Notamify capture of OIIX A1010/26 raw NOTAM. It states A1010/26 NOTAMR A0799/26; Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999; A)OIIX B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST; all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except listed airports operational sunrise to sunset; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; new permission required from CAA for operation of civil IFR passenger flights on those airports. Also admitted ev_16946d327688_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-16946d327688, Indian Express May 23 2026 reporting Iran CAA issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, May 22-May 25 western Tehran FIR/western airspace closure, eight airport exceptions/daylight/fresh approval, commercial rerouting. Weight caveat: Indian Express is secondary and its exception list appears broader/different than raw NOTAM; use it for corroboration and reporting consensus, raw NOTAM controls. Time conversion: May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET = May 25 03:59 UTC (EDT), which falls before NOTAM C)2605250830EST. Legal theory: market rule allows broad closure/suspension involving commercial flights in a major Iranian airspace region; west part of Tehran FIR is a major Iranian airspace region; closure of all airports in that region plus permission suspension/new CAA approval for civil IFR passenger flights is major, non-weather, Iran-initiated. Defense issues: Notamify not direct CAA; QFALC/airport-ops not necessarily overflight/transit; exceptions may mean partial. Response: preponderance plus raw NOTAM and credible report; rule allows limited exceptions; regional airport closures and commercial IFR permission suspension qualify as broad suspension of commercial arrivals/departures in a major region, and Indian Express reports rerouting effect.

Defendant merits work notes. Opportunity `arguments:defendant`, text limit 6000, evidence submission allowed. Re-scanned evidence list and read plaintiff's new evidence. Plaintiff PX-2 `ev_a6870de8ffed_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-a6870de8ffed` is a Notamify capture of OIIX A1010/26. Raw text includes `Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/ A)OIIX B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST` and says all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, and OIAW; previous permissions suspended; new permission required from CAA for civil IFR passenger flights on those airports. Plaintiff PX-3 `ev_16946d327688_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-16946d327688` is Indian Express secondary reporting, partly attributed to Gulf News/NOTAM. It claims western airspace closure and says only eight airports remain operational including Tehran/Mashhad/Isfahan, which conflicts with the raw NOTAM exception identifiers in PX-2.

Defense submitted two focused counter-evidence items. `ev_a516424b54be_submitted-evidence-03-defendant-a516424b54be`: FAA International NOTAM Q-code decode. Q-code groups use second/third letters as subject and fourth/fifth as status; FA = aerodrome; LC = closed. Therefore QFALC = aerodrome closed, not by itself an airspace/FIR overflight closure. `ev_d767617fb81e_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-d767617fb81e`: OurAirports Iran airport code extract. Market-listed airports map to IKA/OIIE, THR/OIII, MHD/OIMM, SYZ/OISS, IFN/OIFM. PX-2 exception list maps to OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW; only OISS/SYZ overlaps with the market-listed airports.

Argument theory: concede PX-2 is timely, non-weather, and likely Iran-origin enough for purposes of argument; focus on whether it meets the market rule. Pathway 1 requires broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major region. PX-2 is an aerodrome-closure NOTAM naming airports and permissions; it does not mention overflights, ATS routes, all commercial transits, or a general FIR airspace ban. Pathway 2 requires complete suspension of commercial arrivals and departures affecting at least two listed airports. Raw PX-2 exception list includes only one listed airport, Shiraz/OISS. Indian Express conflicts with raw NOTAM identifiers and in any event says several listed airports remained operational daylight with approval, which undercuts complete suspension. Plaintiff's secondary report should get less weight than raw NOTAM + FAA decode + airport-code mapping on exact scope.

Search/provenance ledger: read all plaintiff evidence byte ranges and checked metadata. Used web search/open and local curl to retrieve OurAirports CSV; submitted the relevant extracted lines. Used FAA appendix page opened via web for Q-code decode and submitted the relevant captured text. Did not submit SafeAirspace/OpsGroup context because it is not necessary to the textual mismatch and could distract; retained as later leads if operational context becomes decisive. Remaining gap: direct Iranian CAA server/source for A1010/26 not found in time; plaintiff also lacks a direct Iranian server response and relies on Notamify plus secondary reporting.

Rebuttal phase notes. Defendant concedes/does not seriously dispute timeliness, non-weather, and Iran/CAA link; main attack is scope. Read defendant DX-1 ev_a516424b54be: FAA Q-code decode shows FA=Aerodrome, LC=Closed, but defendant's own submitted note says the Q-code does not by itself state route/FIR overflight closure and operative plain text must still be read for scope. Read defendant DX-2 ev_d767617: OurAirports maps OIIE/IKA and OIII/THR to Tehran Province, OIFM/IFN to Isfahan, OISS/SYZ to Shiraz; it says PX-2 exception list includes only OISS/SYZ among the five listed airports, meaning IKA, THR, IFN are not exceptions if they fall in the west-part closure. Rebuttal plan: accept that QFALC is aerodrome-closure coding, but argue the rule does not require a route/FIR overflight ban if official action broadly suspends commercial arrivals/departures in a major Iranian airspace region. PX-2's operative text says ALL AIRPORTS IN WEST PART OF TEHRAN FIR ARE CLSD, all previous permissions suspended, new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. That is a major regional commercial-flight suspension with limited exceptions, which PX-1 says do not disqualify. Use two-airport path only as support; do not overrely because precise 'west part' boundary is not mapped. PX-3's exception-list conflict is a weight issue but it still corroborates CAA issuance, western Tehran FIR closure, and commercial rerouting.

Defendant surrebuttal work notes. Opportunity `surrebuttals:defendant`, text limit 4000. Re-scanned case and evidence. No new source evidence submitted in plaintiff rebuttal; plaintiff used defendant's FAA and airport-code exhibits as PX-R3/PX-R4 and filed a technical rebuttal note. Plaintiff rebuttal points: defense narrowed dispute to scope; QFALC decodes as aerodrome closed but plaintiff says plain text is broad enough because all airports in west Tehran FIR closed except listed daylight/permission operations; plaintiff says market rule does not require every airway/overflight blocked and covers commercial flights arriving/departing from major region with limited exceptions; plaintiff says airport-code point only attacks alternate two-listed-airport path; plaintiff says exception list not including IKA/THR/IFN/MHD may show they were not exceptions, not unaffected.

Surrebuttal theory: accept scope is decisive. Plaintiff's rebuttal rewrites the first pathway by omitting `transiting`; the rule says commercial flights `transiting, arriving in, and departing from` Iranian airspace or a major region. The admitted NOTAM remains airport/aerodrome-focused: QFALC and text `all airports...` plus CAA permission for flights `on these airports`. It does not show transiting commercial flights were suspended, cancelled, or closed across western Tehran FIR. Limited exceptions clause does not cure absent baseline proof. A limited exception to a broad closure differs from deeming an airport-permission regime a broad airspace closure without transit proof. Airport-code point remains relevant because it shows plaintiff's secondary article is unreliable on exact airport list and cannot expand the raw NOTAM. For second pathway, record lacks complete suspension at two listed airports. For first pathway, exact code conflict counsels against stretching the record. No new evidence needed; rely on admitted record and submit narrow surrebuttal.

Closing phase notes. Final record scan used get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. No new evidence after defendant's exhibits; defendant surrebuttal adds argument only. Decisive dispute is scope. Defense concedes or nearly concedes timeliness, non-weather, and Iran/CAA link. Defense says transit through airspace is missing because QFALC is aerodrome-closure code and NOTAM text says airports. Closing plan: emphasize PX-1 rule allows broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights in a major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions do not disqualify; official text closing all airports in west part of Tehran FIR, suspending all prior permissions, and requiring new CAA approval for civil IFR passenger flights is a broad regional commercial-flight suspension even if Q-code is aerodrome closure and even absent a separate airway-closure code. Acknowledge weaknesses: Notamify archive not direct CAA; Indian Express secondary and exception-list conflict; two-listed-airport path not perfect because west boundary not mapped. Argue preponderance is met by raw NOTAM text plus corroboration; defense's reading makes 'arriving/departing' and limited-exception clause too narrow.

Defendant closing work notes. Opportunity `closings:defendant`, text limit 5000. Evidence admission closed. Re-scanned evidence list; unchanged from surrebuttal: market rule, plaintiff Notamify NOTAM, plaintiff Indian Express report, defendant FAA Q-code decode, defendant OurAirports code mapping. Plaintiff closing frames the case as simple: timing/non-weather/Iran initiation mostly uncontested and scope satisfied by all airports in west Tehran FIR closed except limited daylight/permission operations. Plaintiff says QFALC/aerodrome point useful but not controlling because rule includes arrivals/departures in a major region. Plaintiff also says defense offered no contrary Iranian notice or ordinary commercial operations proof.

Closing theory: burden remains plaintiff's; absence of contrary Iranian notice does not expand plaintiff's notice. Concede record shows timely, non-weather, Iran-linked restriction. Unresolved issue is whether it is a `major closure` as PX-1 defines it. First pathway contains `transiting, arriving in, and departing from` and requires broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights, not merely airport closures. PX-2 strongest evidence is QFALC/aerodrome and `all airports...` text, requiring permission for civil IFR passenger flights `on these airports`; it does not prove commercial transit through western FIR was barred. Second pathway fails because complete suspension at two listed airports is not established. Raw exception list overlaps only OISS/SYZ; secondary article conflicts and in any event says named listed airports operational with approval. Provenance/custody: Notamify not direct CAA; Indian Express secondary and partly attributed onward. These are enough for some facts, not enough to stretch scope. Final ask: proposition not proved by preponderance; answer No.
